scope: drift/saturation/alarm cohort reprioritization and global-gate regression audit
learner_facing_quality_improvement: This wave makes learner-facing RAG maintenance more precise by separating a real drift/saturation coverage gap from a new global strict-v3 regression outside the cohort, so the next queue can remove the true blocker before reattempting safe metadata migration.

verification.frontmatter_scan_command: python - <<'PY' ... filename contains drift|saturation|alarm, excluding Pilot lock ... PY
verification.lint_probe_result: all seven sampled frontmatter-present docs still fail the current H1-first authoring lint, so top-of-file v3 metadata remains locally inadmissible in the migrated subset
verification.global_gate_result: the live corpus now fails strict-v3 with 21 violations across three non-Pilot data-structure docs (`arraydeque-vs-blockingqueue-service-handoff-primer.md`, `arraydeque-vs-linkedlist-queue-choice-card.md`, `binary-tree-traversal-routing-guide.md`), which means this lane's drift/saturation cohort is no longer the immediate global blocker
verification.retrieval_probe_result: eval artifacts already reference `anti-corruption-mapping-drift`, `http2-max-concurrent-streams-pending-queue-saturation`, and `permission-model-drift-authz-graph-design`, confirming that learner-facing retrieval demand exists before full v3 admission is restored

priority_non_pilot_targets.target_query_shape: anti corruption mapping drift 뭐예요, external contract drift 왜 생겨요, translation layer drift

priority_non_pilot_targets.target_query_shape: load average 높다, cpu saturation이랑 io wait 어떻게 구분해요, cgroup throttling 먼저 봐야 해요

priority_non_pilot_targets.target_query_shape: permission drift 뭐예요, authz graph design 언제 필요해요, role model drift debugging
priority_non_pilot_targets.why_now: It already appears in cohort eval output, so attaching v3 metadata after the shared gate fix would strengthen a security category that still lacks intermediate coverage.
